events.title: Islamic New Year
events.description: The first day of the Hijri calendar, marking the migration of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from Makkah to Madinah, established during the Caliphate of Umar ibn al-Khattab.

events.title: Arrival of Al-Hussain at Karbala
events.description: Imam Hussain ibn Ali (RA) arrived at the plain of Karbala in 61 AH after being intercepted by a cavalry unit under Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi. He had left Makkah refusing to pledge allegiance to Yazid I, responding to thousands of letters from the people of Kufa promising their support. His caravan of approximately 72 fighters and their families was forced to halt near the Euphrates, far from the safety of Makkah or Madinah. Hussain (RA) made camp, unaware that the Kufan support he had been promised would not materialise — setting the stage for the tragedy of Ashura.

events.title: Water Access Cut in Karbala
events.description: On Muharram 7, the Umayyad army under Umar ibn Sa'd — acting on orders from Ubaidullah ibn Ziyad — blocked Imam Hussain's camp from the Euphrates River, cutting off their water supply. For the final three days before Ashura, the women, children, and companions of Hussain (RA) endured severe thirst in the desert heat while vastly outnumbered. This act is recorded across multiple historical accounts and became one of the most enduring symbols of the injustice at Karbala.

events.title: The Conquest of Khaybar
events.description: The definitive end of the threat from the northern fortresses in 7 AH. It established important precedents for land tax (Kharaj) and treaty law.

events.title: The Day of Ashura
events.description: A day of immense significance: the liberation of Prophet Musa and Bani Israel from Pharaoh, and the martyrdom of Imam Hussain ibn Ali in 61 AH.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Passing of Imam Ali ibn al-Hussain
events.description: The death of the fourth Imam, Zain al-Abidin, known for his piety and the collection of supplications As-Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya.

events.title: Battle of Siffin
events.description: A prolonged confrontation in 37 AH between Caliph Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) and Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, Governor of Syria, on the banks of the Euphrates. It arose from Muawiyah's refusal to pledge allegiance until those responsible for Caliph Uthman's (RA) murder were held accountable. After months of skirmishes, Muawiyah's forces raised copies of the Quran on their spears demanding arbitration, halting the battle. The failed arbitration that followed deepened the political fracture in the Muslim community and contributed to the rise of the Khawarij.
events.notes: The battle spanned Dhul Hijjah 36 AH through Safar 37 AH. Safar marks the month of the battle's climax and ceasefire. The specific day within the month is not definitively recorded in classical sources.

events.title: Birth of Imam Musa al-Kadhim
events.description: Birth of the seventh Imam in the Twelver lineage, known for his restraint of anger and deep scholarship in 128 AH.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Passing of Imam Ali al-Rida
events.description: The eighth Imam passed away in Tus (modern-day Mashhad) in 203 AH.

events.title: Arba'een
events.description: The fortieth day following the martyrdom of Imam Hussain, marking the return of the household of the Prophet to Karbala.

events.title: Wafat of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
events.description: The passing of the Final Messenger of Allah in 11 AH, as observed in Shia tradition. He passed in Madinah after a brief illness.
events.notes: Safar 28 is the date preserved in Shia tradition for the Prophet's passing. The majority of Sunni scholars cite 12 Rabi' al-Awwal 11 AH. Both dates are observed with mourning and remembrance.

events.title: Hijra: Departure from Makkah
events.description: The night Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) left Makkah with Abu Bakr (RA) to the Cave of Thawr to begin the migration.
events.notes: The precise date of departure is debated among classical historians. Most accounts place it at the very end of Safar or the opening days of Rabi' al-Awwal 1 AH. The three-night stay in the Cave of Thawr bridges the two months.

events.title: Laylat al-Mabit
events.description: Ali ibn Abi Talib slept in the Prophet's bed to deceive the assassins while the Prophet (PBUH) migrated.
events.notes: This event occurred the night immediately before the Prophet's departure from Makkah, which classical historians place at the Safar–Rabi' al-Awwal boundary. Rabi' al-Awwal 1 is an approximation; the exact night falls in late Safar by some accounts.

events.title: Passing of Imam Hasan al-Askari
events.description: The death of the eleventh Imam in Samarra in 260 AH.

events.title: Birth of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
events.description: The most widely recognized date for the birth of the Prophet (PBUH) in the Year of the Elephant.

events.title: Arrival at Quba
events.description: The Prophet (PBUH) reached Quba on the outskirts of Madinah and established the first mosque in Islam.

events.title: Wafat of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
events.description: The passing of the Final Messenger of Allah in 11 AH in Madinah, as established by the majority of Sunni scholars. He passed in the house of Aisha (RA) at the age of 63.
events.notes: 12 Rabi' al-Awwal is the most widely accepted date in Sunni scholarship. This same date is also observed as Mawlid an-Nabi — the Prophet's birthday — making it a day of dual significance: both the arrival and the departure of the Final Messenger (PBUH).

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Birth of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
events.description: The date traditionally observed for the birth of the Prophet (PBUH) in Shia history.

events.title: Birth of Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq
events.description: Birth of the sixth Imam and the founder of the Ja'fari school of jurisprudence in 83 AH.

events.title: Construction of Al-Masjid an-Nabawi
events.description: The formal beginning of the construction of the Prophet's Mosque in Madinah.

events.title: Birth of Imam Hasan al-Askari
events.description: Alternative date for the birth of the eleventh Imam in Madinah, 232 AH.

events.title: Passing of Fatima bint Musa
events.description: Death of the daughter of the seventh Imam, known as Bibi Ma'sumah Qom, in 201 AH.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Birth of Zainab bint Ali (RA)
events.description: Birth of the granddaughter of the Prophet (PBUH), known for her eloquence and pivotal role in preserving the message of Islam post-Karbala.

events.title: Battle of Mu'tah
events.description: The first major engagement between the Muslim army and the Byzantine-allied forces in 8 AH, fought near Mu'tah in modern-day Jordan. Three commanders fell in succession: Zayd ibn Haritha (RA), then Ja'far ibn Abi Talib (RA) — cousin of the Prophet — and finally Abdullah ibn Rawaha (RA). Khalid ibn al-Walid (RA) then took command and, through a brilliant tactical withdrawal that deceived the enemy into believing reinforcements had arrived, preserved the Muslim army intact. Upon their return, the Prophet (PBUH) personally conferred upon Khalid the title 'Saifullah' (Sword of Allah).

events.title: Passing of Fatima az-Zahra (RA)
events.description: One of the narrated dates for the passing of the Prophet's (PBUH) daughter in 11 AH, cited in certain historical accounts.
events.notes: The date of Fatima az-Zahra's passing is a matter of historical difference. This date appears in certain narrations. The Shia scholarly consensus places her passing on 3 Jumada al-Thani, 75 days after the Prophet's Wafat.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Birth of Imam Ali ibn al-Hussain (Zain al-Abidin)
events.description: Birth of the fourth Imam in the Ahl al-Bayt lineage, renowned for his asceticism and the collection of prayers known as As-Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya.

events.title: The Battle of Jamal
events.description: A civil conflict in 36 AH near Basra, named after the camel (jamal) upon which Aisha (RA) was mounted. A coalition comprising Talhah ibn Ubaydullah (RA), Zubayr ibn al-Awwam (RA), and Aisha (RA) sought accountability for the murder of Caliph Uthman (RA) before pledging allegiance to Ali (RA). Despite diplomatic efforts, fighting broke out. Ali (RA) emerged victorious; both Talhah (RA) and Zubayr (RA) were killed in battle, while Aisha (RA) was escorted safely back to Madinah with full honour on Ali's (RA) personal instruction — a testament to prophetic etiquette even in civil strife.

events.title: Passing of Fatima az-Zahra (RA)
events.description: The date most widely observed by the Ahl al-Bayt for the martyrdom and passing of the daughter of the Prophet (PBUH).
events.notes: This is the Shia scholarly consensus date, calculated as 75 days after the Prophet's Wafat on 28 Safar. A separate date in Jumada al-Awwal is cited in certain Sunni narrations. The exact date has always been a point of historical difference and should be approached with respect.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Death of Umm ul-Banin
events.description: Passing of Fatima bint Hizam, the wife of Ali ibn Abi Talib and mother of Abbas ibn Ali, known for her immense loyalty and devotion to the family of the Prophet.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Birth of Fatima az-Zahra (RA)
events.description: The birth of the 'Leader of the Women of Paradise' in Makkah, five years before the official start of the Prophetic mission.

events.title: Passing of Caliph Abu Bakr as-Siddiq (RA)
events.description: The death of the first Rightly Guided Caliph and the closest companion of the Prophet (PBUH) in 13 AH.

events.title: Birth of Imam Muhammad al-Baqir
events.description: Birth of the fifth Imam, known as 'the splitter of knowledge' for his vast contributions to Islamic law and science.

events.title: Birth of Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA)
events.description: The birth of the Prophet's cousin, son-in-law, and the fourth Rightly Guided Caliph, in the precinct of the Kaaba in Makkah, 23 years before the Hijra.
events.notes: The detail of birth inside or at the Kaaba is recorded in classical biographical works including Ibn Ishaq, and holds particular significance in Shia tradition. The date of 13 Rajab is widely cited, though some Sunni scholars place his birth in a different month.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Martyrdom of Imam Musa al-Kadhim
events.description: The passing of the seventh Imam in Baghdad after years of imprisonment during the Abbasid Caliphate in 183 AH.

events.title: Al-Isra' wal-Mi'raj
events.description: The miraculous Night Journey of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from Makkah to Jerusalem and his subsequent ascension to the Heavens.

events.title: Liberation of Jerusalem
events.description: In 583 AH (1187 CE), Sultan Salahuddin al-Ayyubi (Saladin) recaptured Jerusalem from the Crusaders after 88 years of occupation, following his decisive victory at the Battle of Hattin. His entry stood in stark contrast to the Crusader conquest of 1099 CE — rather than violence, Salahuddin guaranteed civilian safety, allowed Christian pilgrims to remain, and personally ransomed many captives at his own expense. The Adhan rang again at Al-Masjid al-Aqsa. He remains one of the most celebrated examples of Islamic chivalry and justice in history.

events.title: Birth of Imam Hussain ibn Ali (RA)
events.description: The birth of the Prophet's second grandson and the martyr of Karbala in 4 AH.

events.title: Birth of Abbas ibn Ali (RA)
events.description: Birth of the son of Ali ibn Abi Talib and Umm ul-Banin, celebrated for his peerless bravery and loyalty in 26 AH.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Laylat al-Bara'at (Shab-e-Baraat)
events.description: The Night of Records or Forgiveness, where Muslims pray for divine mercy and the deceased.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Change of the Qibla
events.description: The official shift of the direction of prayer from Masjid al-Aqsa in Jerusalem to the Kaaba in Makkah during 2 AH.

events.title: Birth of Imam Muhammad al-Mahdi
events.description: The birth of the twelfth and final Imam of the Twelver lineage in Samarra, 255 AH.

events.title: Obligation of Sawm (Fasting)
events.description: The formal decree establishing fasting during the month of Ramadan as one of the Five Pillars of Islam, occurring in the second year after Hijra.

events.title: Passing of Umm al-Mu'minin Khadija bint Khuwaylid
events.description: The death of the Prophet Muhammad's (PBUH) first wife and first convert to Islam, marking the beginning of 'Am al-Huzn (The Year of Sorrow).

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Birth of Al-Hasan ibn Ali
events.description: The birth of the eldest son of Ali ibn Abi Talib and Fatimah az-Zahra, and the first grandson of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in 3 AH.

events.title: Nuzool al-Quran (First Revelation)
events.description: The first verses of the Quran were revealed to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) through Jibreel (AS) in the Cave of Hira during Ramadan, approximately 13 years before the Hijra. Jibreel commanded: 'Iqra bismi rabbika alladhi khalaq' — Read in the name of your Lord who created (Quran 96:1). The Prophet (PBUH), overwhelmed, returned to Khadijah (RA) who became the first believer, assuring him: 'By Allah, He will never disgrace you.' This single moment began the 23-year journey of revelation that transformed humanity.
events.notes: The exact night of the first revelation is not definitively known — it fell within the last 10 odd nights of Ramadan. Day 17 is the date most frequently cited in classical biographies such as Ibn Ishaq. This event is spiritually inseparable from Laylat al-Qadr, the Night of Power.

events.title: Ghazwah Badr al-Kubra
events.description: The first major military engagement in Islamic history where a smaller Muslim force defeated the Quraish army, establishing the political and military presence of Islam.

events.title: Fath Makkah (Conquest of Makkah)
events.description: The bloodless re-entry into Makkah by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and 10,000 companions in 8 AH, resulting in the removal of idols from the Kaaba.

events.title: Martyrdom of Ali ibn Abi Talib
events.description: The death of the fourth Rightly Guided Caliph (Khulafa-e-Rashidun) following the assassination attempt by Abd al-Rahman ibn Muljam in Kufa, 40 AH.

events.title: Laylat al-Qadr (The Night of Power)
events.description: The night traditionally observed as the most likely date for the first revelation of the Quran and considered better than a thousand months.

events.title: Eid al-Fitr
events.description: The festival of breaking the fast, marking the end of Ramadan. Established in 2 AH, it is a day of gratitude and congregational prayer (Salat al-Eid).

events.title: Battle of Khandaq (The Trench)
events.description: In Shawwal 5 AH, a massive coalition of Arab and Jewish tribes — known as Al-Ahzab (The Confederates) — besieged Madinah with an estimated 10,000 fighters, dwarfing the Muslim force. Acting on the advice of Salman al-Farisi (RA), the Muslims dug a wide defensive trench along the northern approach to Madinah — a tactic entirely unknown in Arabian warfare. The coalition, unable to cross the trench and weakened by internal disputes and harsh weather, besieged the city for approximately 27 days before withdrawing without breaching the Muslim defences. Allah described the fear and test of that moment, and His decisive intervention, in Surah Al-Ahzab (33:9-27).
events.notes: Classical sources place this battle in Shawwal 5 AH. The specific day of the month is not recorded; this entry represents the beginning of the month in which the siege took place.

events.title: Fasting of the Six Days of Shawwal
events.description: A highly recommended (Mustahabb) act of worship which, when combined with the fasts of Ramadan, is equivalent to fasting for the entire year.

events.title: Battle of Uhud
events.description: The second major military encounter between the Muslims of Madinah and the Meccan Quraish in 3 AH, resulting in significant losses for the Muslims.
events.notes: Classical sources including Ibn Hisham and Ibn Ishaq, as well as Sahih Bukhari's Book of Expeditions (Maghazi), place this battle on 7 Shawwal 3 AH.

events.title: Martyrdom of Hamza ibn Abdul Muttalib
events.description: The death of the Prophet's uncle, known as 'Asadullah' (The Lion of Allah), during the Battle of Uhud in 3 AH.

events.title: The Battle of Hunayn
events.description: Occurred immediately after the Conquest of Makkah in 8 AH. It is mentioned in the Quran (9:25) regarding the lesson of reliance on Allah over pride in numbers.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Passing of Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq
events.description: The death of the sixth Imam of the Ahl al-Bayt and the polymath founder of the Ja'fari school of law in 148 AH.

events.title: Birth of Fatima bint Musa (Al-Ma'sumah)
events.description: The birth of the daughter of the seventh Imam and sister of Imam Ali al-Rida, revered for her scholarship and piety, in 173 AH.

events.title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
events.description: A 10-year peace treaty signed in 6 AH between the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and the Quraysh leadership near Makkah. Though its terms initially appeared disadvantageous to the Muslims — including a clause to return Makkan converts — Allah called it a 'manifest victory' (Quran 48:1). The treaty halted hostilities and opened Arabia to the free spread of Islam, during which tens of thousands embraced the faith. When the Quraysh violated it two years later, it provided the moral justification for the Conquest of Makkah. A masterclass in prophetic statesmanship over short-term emotion.

events.title: Birth of Imam Ali al-Rida
events.description: The birth of the eighth Imam of the Ahl al-Bayt in Madinah, 148 AH, known for his theological debates and role as heir-apparent in the Abbasid court.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Dahw al-Ard
events.description: A day of religious significance marking the traditional spreading of the earth from beneath the Kaaba and the birth of Prophet Ibrahim and Prophet Isa.

events.title: Passing of Imam Muhammad al-Jawad
events.description: The martyrdom of the ninth Imam, also known as At-Taqi, in Baghdad at the age of 25 in 220 AH.

events.title: Marriage of Ali ibn Abi Talib and Fatima az-Zahra
events.description: The sacred union of the Prophet's (PBUH) daughter and cousin in 2 AH, which established the lineage of the Ahl al-Bayt.

events.title: First Ten Days of Dhu al-Hijjah
events.description: A period of high virtue for fasting, remembrance (dhikr), and good deeds, considered superior to all other days.

events.title: Passing of Imam Muhammad al-Baqir
events.description: The death of the fifth Imam in Madinah, 114 AH, a pivotal figure in the codification of Islamic law and traditions.

events.title: Hajjat al-Wada (Farewell Pilgrimage)
events.description: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) performed his only and final complete Hajj in 10 AH, joined by tens of thousands of companions. On the Day of Arafah he delivered the landmark Farewell Sermon (Khutbat al-Wada), establishing foundational principles of human rights, equality, and brotherhood that remain cornerstones of Islamic ethics.

events.title: Day of Arafah
events.description: The pinnacle of the Hajj pilgrimage where pilgrims gather at Mount Arafat to pray and seek forgiveness.

events.title: Eid al-Adha
events.description: The Festival of Sacrifice, commemorating the willingness of Prophet Ibrahim (AS) to sacrifice his son Ishmael in obedience to Allah.

events.title: Ayyam at-Tashriq
events.description: Days of eating and drinking following Eid al-Adha.

events.title: Ayyam at-Tashriq
events.description: Days of eating and drinking following Eid al-Adha.

events.title: Ayyam at-Tashriq
events.description: Days of eating and drinking following Eid al-Adha.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Ayyam al-Bid (White Days)
events.description: Highly recommended fasts of the full moon days.

events.title: Eid al-Ghadir
events.description: The declaration at Ghadir Khumm where Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) announced Ali ibn Abi Talib as the 'Mawla' (leader/master) of the believers in 10 AH.

events.title: Event of Mubahala
events.description: The formal challenge of mutual imprecation between the Prophet (PBUH) and the Christians of Najran in 10 AH, highlighting the status of the Ahl al-Bayt.

events.title: Assassination of Caliph Umar ibn al-Khattab
events.description: The fatal stabbing of the second Rightly Guided Caliph by Piruz Nahavandi (Abu Lu'lu'a) in Madinah in 23 AH.
